Dashboard Archive: 2026-05-20 Home
About This Dashboard
What It Does
Short-term momentum ranker scoring 700+ tickers on returns (1d to 1y), SMA gate (above 30/50/100/200 day), ratio consistency across timeframes, and resilience on bad SPY days. Higher composite scores indicate stronger, more consistent short-term momentum.
Today's Reading
Today's momentum-ranker data shows two exceptionally strong performers at the top of the 700+ ticker universe. MXL leads decisively with a rank-1 score of 96.7, driven by extraordinary returns across all timeframes (703% over one year, 632% over six months) and pristine technical setup—it clears all four major moving averages and maintains a 99.3 score on resilience during SPY weakness. FPLSF ranks second with similarly dominant one-year performance (425%), though its near-term momentum is notably more moderate (3.8% daily, 7.4% weekly) compared to MXL's sharper 1-10 day moves. Both stocks pass ratio consistency checks across timeframes and sit well above their SMAs, but MXL's explosive acceleration pattern (187% in one month, then 430% in three months) is more extreme than FPLSF's steadier climb, suggesting MXL has compressed larger gains into tighter recent windows. The data reveals a market environment where quality momentum candidates are being scored at very high composite levels, with both leaders showing outsized outperformance versus SPY and QQQ over multiple periods.
For educational and informational purposes only. Not financial advice. Past performance does not guarantee future results.
